title: mananthes suratensis
intro: Mananthes suratensis, also known as the flame of the woods or suratensis, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Mananthes suratensis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Mananthes suratensis is traditionally prepared into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and poultices, which are used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
